text:
Hey,

I can't believe the day is actually here. It feels like just yesterday we were talking about this like it was some far-off possibility, and now you're really going. I'd be lying if I said I wasn't a little sad about it, but honestly, I'm mostly just so excited for you and everything that's waiting for you on the other side of this move.

You're going to land on your feet, I have absolutely no doubt about that. You have this way of walking into a new situation and making it feel like home within weeks, and I've always admired that about you. New city, new neighbors, new favorite coffee spot — you'll have it all figured out before the rest of us even realize you're gone.

Just promise me we won't become one of those pairs of friends who say "we'll visit soon" for three years and then suddenly realize they haven't actually seen each other. I'm serious. I'm already looking at your calendar.

Thank you for everything you've brought to my life. The dinners, the long conversations, the times you talked me down from my own worst ideas, and the times you enthusiastically joined me in them anyway. That stuff doesn't just go away because the distance changes.

Go have an adventure. Just don't forget about us back here rooting for you.

With so much love,

David
